# SaaSyBase

SaaSyBase is a production-ready SaaS boilerplate for vibecoders and experienced developers. It provides the infrastructure layer you usually do not want to build from scratch: auth, billing, organizations, tokens, admin dashboards, email, notifications, support, analytics, and ops tooling.

## Read This First

- This codebase is scaffold-first, not app-logic-first. Your product code belongs mainly in `app/dashboard/`, `components/`, and `lib/`.
- Auth and payments are provider-agnostic. Use `authService` and `PaymentProviderFactory`; do not import vendor SDKs in business logic.
- Many flows are already wired: checkout, webhooks, subscriptions, organization provisioning, token spending, email templates, and admin tooling.
- The canonical docs are `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `PATTERNS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `TECH_STACK.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## Fast Setup

```bash
cp .env.example .env.local
npm install
npx prisma migrate dev --name init
npx prisma db seed
npm run dev
```

Use `AUTH_PROVIDER="clerk"` or `AUTH_PROVIDER="nextauth"`. Use `PAYMENT_PROVIDER="stripe"`, `"paystack"`, `"paddle"`, or `"razorpay"`.

## What the App Ships

- Dual auth provider system with Clerk organizations or NextAuth credentials/OAuth/email login.
- Four active payment providers plus centralized webhook ingress.
- Paid, free, and shared token pools with organization-aware consumption.
- Team plan provisioning, invites, member management, and teardown.
- Feature gates, coupons, subscriptions, invoices, and refund receipts.
- Blog CMS, site pages, theming, email templates, notifications, and support tickets.
- Admin dashboard sections for users, plans, billing, content, support, analytics, and maintenance.
- Demo read-only mode for safe showcase environments.

## Codebase Map

- `app/` — App Router pages and API routes.
- `components/` — Reusable UI and domain components.
- `lib/` — Business logic, provider abstractions, settings, tokens, teams, billing, email, and security helpers.
- `prisma/` — Schema and seed data.
- `tests/` — Vitest and Playwright coverage.
- `docs/` — Deeper implementation notes for billing, orgs, and token handling.

## High-Value Rules for Agents

- Use `authService.requireUserId()` in protected API routes.
- Use `handleApiError()` in API `catch` blocks.
- Validate inputs with Zod before touching business logic.
- Rate-limit public endpoints.
- Use `Logger` instead of `console.log`.
- Check provider capability support before calling optional methods.
- Query provider identifiers through provider-neutral fields and JSON map helpers.
- Use the Prisma singleton from `lib/prisma.ts`.

## Key Routes

- `/dashboard` — user dashboard and shipped demo workspace.
- `/dashboard/team` — team management.
- `/dashboard/notifications` — notification center.
- `/dashboard/support` — support tickets.
- `/admin` — admin dashboard.
- `/admin/organizations` — organization browser.
- `/admin/maintenance` — maintenance tools and mode toggle.
- `/api/webhooks/payments` — centralized payment webhook ingress.
- `/api/cron/process-expiry` — expiry automation.
- `/api/health` — health check.

## Docs Index

- `README.md` — product-facing overview and setup.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — system diagrams and data flow.
- `PATTERNS.md` — implementation patterns to follow.
- `CLAUDE.md` — agent rules and quick reference.
- `INSTRUCTIONS.md` — developer workflow and conventions.
- `AGENTS.md` — specialized agent roles.
- `TECH_STACK.md` — stack, versions, and rationale.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — contribution workflow.
- `docs/adding-payment-providers.md` — payment provider extension guide.
- `docs/token-usage-and-deduction.md` — token spend API guide.
- `docs/organization-rollout-plan.md` — organization rollout status and history.

## Useful Environment Groups

- Core: `DATABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_DOMAIN`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_NAME`
- Auth: `AUTH_PROVIDER`, `AUTH_SECRET`, Clerk keys, NextAuth OAuth keys
- Payments: `PAYMENT_PROVIDER`, `PAYMENTS_CURRENCY`, provider secrets, webhook secrets
- Security: `ENCRYPTION_SECRET`, `INTERNAL_API_TOKEN`, `HEALTHCHECK_TOKEN`, `CRON_PROCESS_EXPIRY_TOKEN`
- Email: `EMAIL_PROVIDER`, `SMTP_*`, `RESEND_API_KEY`, `EMAIL_FROM`, `SUPPORT_EMAIL`
- Storage and analytics: `LOGO_*`, `AWS_*`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID`, `GA_*`

## Practical Note

The shipped product is a foundation. If you are building a new SaaS on top of it, keep the infra layers intact and add your product-specific logic where the route and component structure already expects it.
